metadata.source_paper: Shih, H.-C., Heliani, L. S., Hsiao, Y.-S., Hwang, C., & Pahlevi, A. M. (2026). A case study of geoid modeling in Sulawesi and accuracy verification strategies for accommodating diverse MSL vertical datums. Terrestrial, Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences, 37, 1. https://doi.org/10.1007/s44195-025-00119-4
metadata.comprehension_summary: Geoid modeling para Sulawesi usando gravimetria aerea BIG+DTU (2008-2019) + 57 puntos de gravedad absoluta (A10 gravimeter). Mejora precision geoidal de nivel metro (INAGEOID2020) a nivel decimetro. Residuos >0.5 m en NE offshore. Fig. 1 muestra distribucion de estaciones en Indonesia.
main_conclusions: Geoid accuracy improved from meter-level to decimeter-level for Sulawesi
57 absolute gravity points (A10 gravimeter, BIG) distributed across Indonesia
Airborne gravity surveys 2008-2019 (BIG+DTU) fill gaps in complex terrain
Residual geoid differences >0.5 m in nearshore/offshore NE Sulawesi — complex gravity field in collision zone
figures_used: Fig. 1 (distribution absolute + terrestrial + airborne gravity datasets)
metadata.nota_estaciones: 57 estaciones A10 distribuidas en toda Indonesia. ~14-15 visibles en Sulawesi en Fig. 1 (escala Indonesia-wide, precision +/-0.3 grados). Posiciones en ciudades principales accesibles por carretera.

properties.name: Area de estudio — Modelado geoidal Sulawesi (Shih 2026)
properties.paper_finding: Sulawesi geoid improved to decimeter accuracy; 57 A10 stations across Indonesia; airborne surveys 2008-2019
properties.extraction_notes: Extent del area de estudio tomado del texto del paper.

conclusions.text: Geoid height residuals >0.5 m in nearshore/offshore NE Sulawesi reflect extreme density contrasts in the collision zone
conclusions.geodyn_implication: Los residuos geoidales en el NE offshore reflejan la complejidad gravitatoria de la doble subduccion Molucas — variaciones de densidad cortical extremas dificultan el modelado geoidal

properties.name: Estaciones gravedad absoluta BIG — Sulawesi (~14 de 57 totales)
properties.full_name: BIG Absolute Gravity Stations A10 — Sulawesi subset (~14 stations), read from Fig. 1
properties.paper_finding: ~14 A10 stations visible in Sulawesi from Fig. 1; distributed at major cities accessible by road
properties.extraction_notes: Coordenadas de ciudades principales leidas visualmente de Fig. 1 (escala Indonesia-wide). Precision ~0.2-0.3 grados. Las 57 coordenadas exactas no estan en el texto del paper.

conclusions.text: ~14 A10 absolute gravity stations in Sulawesi distributed at major accessible cities — coverage limited by complex terrain and infrastructure gaps
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La cobertura de estaciones absolutas sigue el patron de accesibilidad (ciudades costeras y centros urbanos) — el interior montanoso de Sulawesi queda cubierto solo por la gravimetria aerea

properties.name: Cobertura gravimetria aerea Sulawesi 2008 (BIG+DTU)
properties.full_name: Airborne gravity survey coverage Sulawesi Island 2008 — BIG + Technical University of Denmark
properties.purpose: Fill gravity data gaps in complex terrain of Sulawesi; input to geoid modeling
properties.paper_finding: 2008 airborne gravity survey covers entire Sulawesi Island — visible as blue shaded area in Fig. 1
properties.extraction_notes: Poligono de cobertura aerogravimetrica aproximado desde Fig. 1 (zona azul sombreada sobre Sulawesi).

conclusions.text: 2008 airborne gravity survey (BIG+DTU) provides complete coverage of Sulawesi terrain inaccessible to ground surveys
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La cobertura aerogravimetrica de Sulawesi es el dataset de referencia para la estructura gravimetrica regional — base para S4 del mapa
